channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇱🇻 Latvian Defense Minister Resigns After Drone Incursions
item.description: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Silina requested the resignation of Defense Minister Andris Spruds after drones violated the country's airspace, saying he had lost her trust and "that of the public."

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Unveils New Yildirimhan ICBM With 6,000km Range
item.description: Turkey revealed a previously unknown intercontinental ballistic missile, raising major questions about Ankara's strategic ambitions and defense capabilities in the region.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Strikes Iron Dome Battery With FPV Drone
item.description: Hezbollah used an FPV drone to strike an Iron Dome battery. The IDF has struggled to respond to FPV drone threats that use special cables and manual operation to evade jamming technologies.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Wanted Former Polish Justice Minister Flees Hungary
item.description: Zbigniew Ziobro fled Hungary after newly sworn-in Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he would extradite him. Ziobro told Polish outlet TV Republica he left as Magyar takes power.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Confirms Trump State Visit to Beijing This Week
item.description: China confirmed dates for President Donald Trump's state visit to Beijing, the first trip to the country by a US president since 2017. The visit includes two bilateral meetings amid tensions over Iran, tariffs, Taiwan and tech rivalry.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Rejects Iran Ceasefire Proposal as "Totally Unacceptable"
item.description: US President dismissed Iran's response to American proposals aimed at ending the conflict, keeping the Strait of Hormuz effectively shut and oil prices elevated.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱 Israeli Strikes Kill 51 in Lebanon, Including Medics, as Ceasefire Unravels
item.description: Israeli attacks intensify in southern Lebanon with 552 killed since the ceasefire started on April 16, including at least 51 in the past 24 hours.

item.title: 🇵🇱🇭🇺 Polish Ex-Minister Flees Hungary to US After PM Magyar Refuses Protection
item.description: Former Polish minister sought refuge in the United States after Hungary's prime minister said the country would not protect individuals wanted elsewhere.

item.title: 🇬🇷 Greece Investigates Explosive-Packed Drone Found in Territorial Waters
item.description: Greek officials are scrambling to explain how a drone carrying explosives ended up in their waters, with suspicion falling on the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi Granted Bail, Transferred to Hospital
item.description: Iranian authorities released the ailing human rights activist on bail amid growing concern over her deteriorating health in prison.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi Granted Bail and Hospital Transfer in Iran
item.description: Iranian authorities granted human rights activist Narges Mohammadi bail amid growing concern over her poor health in prison. The jailed Iranian Nobel laureate faced a medical crisis.

item.title: 💰🛢️ Oil Prices Jump After Trump Dismisses Iran Proposal
item.description: Global oil prices surged as the Strait of Hormuz waterway remains effectively shut, severely disrupting global energy shipments. The standoff continues between US and Iran.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇦 Putin Hints Ukraine Conflict "Coming to an End" Amid Ceasefire Talks
item.description: The Russian leader sees potential for Ukraine negotiations but condemns the West backing for President Zelensky. A US-backed ceasefire is in effect.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇦 Russia Kills Three Ukrainians in 24 Hours, Accuses Kyiv of Violating Truce
item.description: Russia claims Ukraine has committed more than 1,000 violations since the three-day truce came into effect. Russian strikes killed three and injured others despite the ceasefire.

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Russia Breaks Ceasefire as Strikes Kill 1, Injure 19 Across Ukraine
item.description: Russian attacks during the declared three-day ceasefire killed one civilian and injured 19 others across Ukraine. Ukraine's Air Force intercepted all 27 drones, but fighting continued on the front lines with 147 combat clashes recorded.

item.title: 🇱🇻🚁 Latvian Defense Minister Resigns After Drone Incursions
item.description: Latvian Defense Minister Andris Spruds resigned after drones violated Latvia's airspace. Prime Minister Evika Silina requested his resignation, citing loss of trust.

item.title: 🇵🇱🔍 Poland Finds Military Spy Drone Near Russia's Kaliningrad Border
item.description: Poland's military police found what appears to be a military reconnaissance drone near the border with Russia's Kaliningrad exclave. The drone had no combat capabilities according to preliminary findings.

item.title: 🇬🇧🏥 British Paratroopers Land on Remote Island for Hantavirus Case
item.description: A specialist British military team parachuted onto Tristan da Cunha, one of the world's most remote inhabited islands, to treat a suspected hantavirus case. The operation highlights global health concerns as a cruise ship outbreak continues.

item.title: 🇵🇱🇭🇺 Polish Ex-Minister Flees Hungary to US After PM Warning
item.description: Zbigniew Ziobro, former Polish justice minister accused of leading an organized criminal enterprise, fled to the US after Hungary's new PM said the country would not protect people wanted elsewhere.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇮🇶 Israel Operated Secret Military Base in Iraq During Iran War
item.description: Israel established a clandestine outpost in the Iraqi desert to support its air campaign against Iran, housing special forces and serving as a logistics hub. Israel launched airstrikes against Iraqi troops who nearly discovered it early in the war.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's Supreme Leader Missing and Silent as War Negotiations Continue
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since being severely injured in a February airstrike that killed his father Ali Khamenei. Only AI-generated images and purported written messages have emerged, raising questions about who is actually running Iran.

item.title: 🛢️💰 Saudi Aramco Profit Jumps 25% as Pipeline Bypasses Hormuz Disruption
item.description: The world's largest oil company reported $32.5 billion in Q1 profit as it shifted exports to its East-West Pipeline running to the Red Sea. Brent crude stands at $103.91/barrel, below war highs but still elevated from pre-war $70 levels.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey announces propellant breakthrough for Yıldırımhan long-range ballistic missile
item.description: The Turkish government has achieved advances in hypersonic technologies and liquid-propellant propulsion systems for its Yıldırımhan missile program after nearly a decade of development.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇸 Kremlin expects US envoys Witkoff and Kushner for peace talks quite soon
item.description: Russian officials said they expect US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to visit Moscow soon. Peace talks remain stalled as the Kremlin insists Ukraine must withdraw from Donbas first.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary's New PM Apologizes to Those Wronged Under Orbán
item.description: Hungary's newly elected prime minister delivered an apology to citizens harmed during Viktor Orbán's 14-year rule, marking a dramatic shift in the country's political direction after the opposition's victory.
